segments.caption: The video opens with a close-up of hands holding a ceramic palette adorned with delicate pink and green floral designs, showcasing its intricate details. The scene transitions to a hand using a wooden rolling pin to flatten a piece of clay on a cloth-covered surface, followed by a brush applying a dark liquid to the clay, possibly a slip or glaze. The focus then shifts to a hand pouring pink ceramic powder from a small glass jar into a ceramic palette, which already contains a green leaf design. The video continues with a hand opening a wooden drawer, revealing an organized collection of ceramic tools, including loop tools and a metal scraper, which are then placed on a wooden board. The setting is a well-lit, warm-toned wooden workspace with various ceramic pieces and tools visible in the background. The hands then handle several sealed plastic bags containing different colored powders—pink, green, and blue—placing them on a gray cutting mat marked with grid lines. The powders are poured into clear glass jars, one by one, with the pink powder being transferred first, followed by green and then blue. On-screen text appears, stating, 'In this video, I wanted to show you the steps of my ceramic products that I made for myself,' and later, 'I will not be including detailed technical information because I am not a professional in this field.' The hands are seen wearing a light gray sweater, and the workspace remains consistently organized and aesthetically pleasing throughout the segment.

segments.caption: The video segment captures the meticulous process of assembling a ceramic vessel using the coil or slab-building technique. The hands, with light purple nail polish, begin by applying a thin layer of slip (a watery clay mixture) to the scored edge of a circular clay base using a small brush. This prepares the surface for adhesion. Next, a curved slab of clay is carefully positioned and pressed onto the base, forming the first wall of the vessel. The hands then continue adding more curved clay slabs, aligning them edge-to-edge and pressing them firmly to ensure a strong bond. After each addition, the seams are smoothed and refined using a metal tool with a wooden handle, which scrapes and blends the joints both externally and internally. The interior of the vessel is also smoothed with a loop tool to remove excess clay and create a clean, even surface. The workspace is covered with a textured white cloth, showing signs of use with clay smudges, and the wooden rolling pin is visible in the background, indicating prior preparation of the clay slabs. The potted plant and glass bowl remain in the background, contributing to the calm, natural atmosphere of the craft environment. The process emphasizes precision, patience, and attention to detail in building a sturdy, seamless ceramic form.
